extracted.outside_date_extension_condition: Antitrust or Section 7.01(b) or 7.01(c) conditions remain unmet but other Article VII conditions are satisfied or waivable; extensible in three-month increments
extracted.voting_threshold: majority of outstanding shares of Penumbra, Inc. common stock
regulatory_approvals_required: Foreign antitrust and foreign investment law approvals
extracted.regulatory_filing_deadlines: HSR Act filings within 20 Business Days of Agreement date; regulatory processes initiated within 50 calendar days
extracted.dividend_allowed: Penumbra, Inc.: no dividends except intra-subsidiary distributions; Boston Scientific Corporation: no dividends except as required by law with prior written consent of Penumbra, Inc.
extracted.financing_condition: No financing condition; Boston Scientific Corporation and Pinehurst Merger Sub represent they will have sufficient funds at Effective Time to fulfill all obligations including paying aggregate Cash Consideration
conditions_to_closing: Penumbra, Inc. stockholder approval (majority of outstanding shares)
Expiration or termination of HSR Act waiting periods and receipt of all requisite regulatory approvals
Form S-4 effectiveness without stop order
NYSE approval for listing of Boston Scientific Corporation shares
Accuracy of specified representations and warranties as of Agreement and Closing Dates without Material Adverse Effect
Material compliance with covenants by both parties
No Material Adverse Effect since Agreement date
Delivery of closing officer certificates confirming satisfaction of conditions
specific_termination_triggers: Outside date (January 14, 2027, extendable to January 14, 2028) if Merger has not occurred, unless terminating party caused delay
Final, nonappealable governmental order prohibiting the Merger
Failure to obtain Penumbra, Inc. stockholder approval
Boston Scientific Corporation termination if Penumbra, Inc. Board effects Adverse Recommendation Change
Penumbra, Inc. termination upon entering into Superior Proposal (with $525,000,000 termination fee payment)
Boston Scientific Corporation termination for uncured material breach by Penumbra, Inc. (30-day cure period)
Penumbra, Inc. termination for uncured material breach by Boston Scientific Corporation or Merger Sub (30-day cure period)
Termination due to failure to obtain regulatory approvals related to Antitrust Laws by Outside Date triggers $900,000,000 reverse termination fee
extracted.interim_operating_covenants: Penumbra, Inc. shall conduct business only in ordinary course, preserve business organization and material assets, maintain material licenses and permits, keep available executive officers and key employees, and maintain material business relationships. Penumbra, Inc. and subsidiaries prohibited from declaring or paying dividends except intra-subsidiary distributions. Boston Scientific Corporation restricted from amending Charter or By-laws or taking specified actions except as required by law or with prior written consent of Penumbra, Inc.

source_text:
Merger Agreement Clause Summaries

Concise Summary
Presigning
+ Confidentiality Agreement dated December 19, 2025.

○ References: Section 9.03
○ From : Confidentiality and Clean Room - Concise - 1.1

+ The board of directors of Penumbra, Inc. has unanimously approved the merger and
recommended it to stockholders; the board of directors of Boston Scientific Corporation has
unanimously authorized and approved the merger.
○ References: Section 4.12; Preamble; Section 3.24
○ From : Board Approval - Concise - 1.2

Consideration
+ Penumbra, Inc. stockholders are entitled to receive, at their election, either $374.00 in cash or
3.8721 Boston Scientific Corporation shares per Penumbra, Inc. share, with fractional shares
paid in cash and any shares without election receiving Merger Consideration as determined in
the agreement.
○ References: Section 2.01 (a) (a)
○ From : Consideration Details - Concise - 2.1

+ Penumbra, Inc. and its subsidiaries are not permitted to pay dividends prior to closing except
intra-subsidiary distributions.
○ References: Section 5.01 (b) (iii)
○ From : Target Dividends - Concise - 2.2

+ Boston Scientific Corporation is not permitted to declare or pay any dividends prior to closing.

○ References: Section 5.02
○ From : Parent Dividends - Concise - 2.3

+ Boston Scientific Corporation will allocate Cash and Stock Consideration rights among
Penumbra, Inc. holders by prorating Cash Election Shares if exceeding 73.26% and allocating
Shortfall Number among Non-Election and Stock Election Shares per detailed fractions.
○ References: Section 2.02
○ From : Proration - Concise - 2.4

+ The agreement includes a termination fee payable by Penumbra, Inc. or Boston Scientific
Corporation upon termination under specified conditions, with amounts of $525,000,000 or
$900,000,000 depending on the party, but it does not describe a per-day or per-share ticking fee
for deal delays.
○ References: Section 8.03

○ From : Ticking Fees - Concise - 2.7

Conditions & Financing
+ Subject to the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of all outstanding shares of Penumbra,
Inc. common stock to adopt this Agreement.
○ References: Section 7.01; ; Section 3.24 (b)
○ From : Conditions to Closing : Highlight Conditions - 3.1

+ The closing is conditioned on expiration of the HSR Act waiting period and receipt of all other
required antitrust approvals.
○ References: Section 7.01 (c)
○ From : Conditions to Closing : Regulatory Clearances Required - 3.2

+ Committed financing was not included in the merger contract.

○ References: Section 4.11
○ From : Financing Summary : Committed Financing - Concise - 3.4

Regulatory
+ The outside date is January 14, 2027, extendable by Boston Scientific Corporation or Penumbra,
Inc. in three-month increments up to January 14, 2028 if certain antitrust or Section 7.01
conditions remain unmet, with termination rights restricted if a party caused delay.
○ References: Section 8.01
○ From : Outside Date - Concise - 4.1
+ Parent: Boston Scientific Corporation
Merger Sub: Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.
Company: Penumbra, Inc.
Boston Scientific Corporation must pay Penumbra, Inc. $900,000,000 if termination occurs due
to failure to obtain a Requisite Regulatory Approval under Antitrust Laws.
○ References: Section 8.03 (b) (iv)
○ From : RTF for Regulatory - Concise - 4.2

+ Boston Scientific Corporation is required to proffer divestitures and remedies per Section 6.06(d)
and Parent Disclosure Schedule, conditioned on consummation; Penumbra, Inc. may divest,
hold separate or limit businesses only with Parent’s prior written consent.
○ References: Section 6.06 (d)
○ From : Best Efforts : Remedy Actions - 4.3

+ Boston Scientific Corporation is required to defend against any litigation.

○ References: Section 6.06 (d)
○ From : Best Efforts : Litigation Commitments - 4.5

+ Boston Scientific Corporation shall have the sole right to determine, direct, and implement the
strategy and timing for obtaining antitrust and regulatory approvals, including pulling and refiling

HSR filings; however, pulling and refiling more than once requires prior written consent of
Penumbra, Inc., and Boston Scientific Corporation must consult in good faith with Penumbra,
Inc. regarding these matters.
○ References: Section 6.06 (b)
○ From : Best Efforts : Right to Control Strategy - 4.6

+ Boston Scientific Corporation shall have the sole right to control timing and strategy for obtaining
antitrust and regulatory approvals, including pulling/refiling HSR filings (more than once requires
prior written consent of Penumbra, Inc.), and shall consult in good faith with Penumbra, Inc.
○ References: Section 6.06 (b)
○ From : Best Efforts : Right to Control Timing - 4.7

Competing Bid - Company
+ In the Pre-Closing Period, Penumbra, Inc. and its subsidiaries must not solicit, initiate, facilitate,
or encourage Acquisition Proposals except as permitted, with specified notification and fiduciary
duty exceptions.
○ References: Section 6.03 (b)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Go Shop - 5.1

+ Penumbra, Inc. must pay Boston Scientific Corporation $525,000,000 if terminated for a superior
offer.
○ References: Section 8.03 (b) (i)
○ From : Termination for Superior Bid - Concise - 5.3

+ Penumbra, Inc. must reimburse Boston Scientific Corporation for all reasonable expenses
incurred collecting any overdue termination fee if Penumbra, Inc. fails to pay the $525,000,000
fee triggered by specified termination events, but no reimbursement is tied solely to failure to
obtain shareholder approval.
○ References: Section 8.03 (f)
○ From : Reimbursement Expenses Identified - Concise - 5.4

Competing Bid - Parent
+ Penumbra, Inc. has a four (4) calendar day right-to-negotiate any Parent Superior Proposal; and
two (2) calendar days for any subsequent amended proposals.
○ References: Section 6.03 (e)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Right To Negotiate Concise - 5.12

+ The Penumbra, Inc. shall pay Boston Scientific Corporation $525,000,000 as the Company
Termination Fee if termination occurs upon failure to obtain shareholder approval linked to an
Acquisition Proposal; Boston Scientific Corporation bears no reimbursement obligation.
○ References: Section 8.03 (f)
○ From : Parent Reimbursement Expenses Identified - Concise - 5.14

Other Items

+ Penumbra, Inc. shall use reasonable best efforts to conduct its business only in the ordinary
course, and Boston Scientific Corporation must not delay or impede consummation using
reasonable best efforts.
○ References: Section 5.01 (a)
○ From : Ordinary Course Covenant - Concise - 6.2

+ The MAE carve-outs in the Agreement exclude events arising from changes in general
economic, political, regulatory, business, financial, credit, or capital market conditions; industry-
wide changes; GAAP/legal changes; disease outbreaks or pandemics; political conditions
including war or terrorism; announcement or pendency of the Transactions; compliance with the
Agreement; Transaction Litigation; changes in trading price/volume or ratings; and failure to
meet projections. MAE carve-outs appear standard.
○ References: Section 9.03
○ From : MAE Non Standard Carveouts - Concise - 6.3

+ Specific performance is available to Boston Scientific Corporation, Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.,
and Penumbra, Inc.
○ References: Section 9.10
○ From : Specific Performance - Concise - 6.4

+ This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the State of
Delaware.
○ References: Section 9.11
○ From : Law and Jurisdiction : Governing Law - Concise - 6.5

+ Matters relating to this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the
Laws of the State of Delaware.
○ References: Section 9.11
○ From : Law and Jurisdiction : Financing Jurisdiction - Concise - 6.6

Fulsome Summary
Party Details
+ Company is Penumbra, Inc., a Delaware corporation.
○ References: ARTICLE Preamble
○ From : Party Details : Target - 1.1

+ Acquirer is Boston Scientific Corporation, a Delaware corporation.

○ References: ARTICLE Preamble
○ From : Party Details : Acquirer - 1.2

+ The transaction is a reverse triangular merger where Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc. merges into
Penumbra, Inc., with Penumbra, Inc. surviving as the surviving corporation and becoming a
wholly owned subsidiary of Boston Scientific Corporation.
○ References: Section 1.01

○ From : Party Details : Transaction - 1.3

+ Boston Scientific Corporation, a Delaware corporation, guarantees Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.,
its wholly owned Delaware subsidiary, under this Agreement.
○ References: Section 6.07
○ From : Party Details : Guarantor - 1.4

+ Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc., a Delaware corporation, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Boston
Scientific Corporation. It is formed to merge with Penumbra, Inc., ceasing its separate existence
upon merger.
○ References: ARTICLE Preamble
○ From : Party Details : Merger Sub - 1.5

Board Approval
+ The board of directors of Penumbra, Inc. unanimously approved the merger and related
transactions; the board of directors of Boston Scientific Corporation unanimously authorized and
approved the merger and related transactions.
○ References: Section 4.12; Preamble; Section 3.24
○ From : Board Approval - Fulsome - 2.1

Closing
+ The Closing Date for the merger between Boston Scientific Corporation, Pinehurst Merger Sub,
Inc., and Penumbra, Inc. is 8:00 a.m. New York time on the fifth Business Day after all Article VII
conditions are satisfied or waived in writing.
○ References: Section 1.02
○ From : Target Closing - 3.1
Consideration and Dividends
+ Penumbra, Inc. stockholders are entitled to receive, at their election, either $374.00 in cash or
3.8721 Boston Scientific Corporation shares per Penumbra, Inc. share, with fractional shares
paid in cash and the right to receive such Merger Consideration as determined for non-election
shares.
○ References: Section 2.01 (a) (a)
○ From : Consideration Details - Fulsome - 4.1

+ Boston Scientific Corporation must pay Penumbra, Inc. a $900,000,000 Parent Termination Fee
within two Business Days after termination under specified sections, and Penumbra, Inc. must
pay Boston Scientific Corporation a $525,000,000 Company Termination Fee within two
Business Days after certain Acquisition Proposal events.
○ References: Section 3.15 (a) (ix)
○ From : Contingent Payments - Fulsome - 4.2

+ Penumbra, Inc. and its subsidiaries are not permitted to declare, set aside, make, or pay any
dividends or distributions prior to closing, except for dividends between wholly owned
subsidiaries.

○ References: Section 5.01 (b) (iii)
○ From : Target Dividends - Fulsome - 4.3

+ Boston Scientific Corporation is not permitted to declare, set aside, or pay any dividends prior to
closing except as required by law, with prior written consent of Penumbra, Inc., or as otherwise
expressly allowed.
○ References: Section 5.02
○ From : Parent Dividends - Fulsome - 4.4

+ The Merger Consideration payable by Boston Scientific Corporation shall be adjusted if, between
January 14, 2026, and the Effective Time, the outstanding shares of Penumbra, Inc. or Boston
Scientific Corporation change in number or class due to stock dividends, splits, or similar events,
to preserve the same economic effect as originally contemplated.
○ References: Section 2.07
○ From : Dividend Adjustment Mechanism - Fulsome - 4.5

Conditions
+ The mutual conditions to closing the Merger among Boston Scientific Corporation, Pinehurst
Merger Sub, Inc., and Penumbra, Inc. include: obtaining Penumbra, Inc.’s stockholder approval;
the absence of any order from a Governmental Authority prohibiting the consummation of the
Merger; expiration or termination of all applicable waiting periods under the HSR Act and receipt
of all required governmental consents and approvals; effectiveness of the Form S-4 without any
stop order; and approval of listing of Boston Scientific Corporation’s shares to be issued in the
Merger on the New York Stock Exchange.
○ References: Section 7.01
○ From : Conditions to Closing : Mutual Conditions List - 5.1

+ The Parent’s obligation to close is conditioned upon obtaining Company stockholder approval;
absence of prohibitive governmental orders; expiration or termination of HSR waiting periods and
receipt of all requisite regulatory approvals; effectiveness of the Form S-4 without stop orders;
NYSE approval for listing Parent Shares; accuracy of specified Company representations and
warranties as of the Agreement and Closing Dates without material adverse effect; the
Company’s material compliance with covenants; no occurrence of a Company Material Adverse
Effect since the Agreement date; and delivery of a closing officer certificate confirming
satisfaction of these conditions.
○ From : Conditions to Closing : Parent Closing Conditions - 5.2

+ The obligations of Penumbra, Inc. to consummate the merger with Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.
are subject to the satisfaction or waiver of conditions including that Boston Scientific
Corporation’s specified representations and warranties are true and correct as of the Closing
Date, Boston Scientific Corporation and Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc. have materially complied
with their respective covenants, no Parent Material Adverse Effect has occurred since the
Agreement date, and Boston Scientific Corporation delivers a Closing Date officer certificate
confirming these conditions are met.
○ References: Section 7.03

○ From : Conditions to Closing : Company Closing Conditions - 5.3

+ The parties are:
Parent = Boston Scientific Corporation
Merger Sub = Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc.
Company = Penumbra, Inc.
Subject to the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of all outstanding shares of Penumbra,
Inc. common stock approving the Agreement, which is the sole required vote to approve and
consummate the merger. The excerpts do not specify any voting threshold for Boston Scientific
Corporation.
○ References: Section 7.01
○ From : Conditions to Closing : Shareholder Approval - Fulsome - 5.4

Proxy, Shareholder Vote, Dissenting Shares
+ Following the Agreement date and no later than 30 Business Days thereafter, Boston Scientific
Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. shall jointly prepare and file the Proxy Statement/Prospectus,
and Boston Scientific Corporation shall prepare and file the Form S-4.
○ References: Section 6.01 (a)
○ From : Filing Timing - Fulsome - 6.1

+ Boston Scientific Corporation, with Pinehurst Merger Sub, shall use reasonable best efforts to
have the Form S-4 declared effective promptly after filing, including responding to SEC
comments, with Pinehurst Merger Sub assisting Penumbra, Inc. as reasonably requested. The
proxy statement shall be jointly prepared by Boston Scientific Corporation and Pinehurst Merger
Sub within 30 Business Days of the Agreement date.
○ References: Section 6.01
○ From : Effectiveness Efforts - Fulsome - 6.2

+ Boston Scientific Corporation shall cause Penumbra, Inc. to call, give notice of, convene and
hold the Company Stockholders' Meeting as promptly as reasonably practicable following the
effectiveness of the Form S-4, not initially scheduled later than thirty-five (35) days following
such effectiveness.
○ References: Section 6.01 (b)
○ From : Shareholder Meeting - Fulsome - 6.3

+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. shall hold the shareholder meeting no later
than 35 days after Form S-4 effectiveness. Adjournments/postponements allowed if no quorum,
legal requirements, supplemental disclosure, or proxy solicitation; limited to 20 Business Days
total and no later than 5 Business Days before the Outside Date, without Boston Scientific
Corporation’s prior written consent.
○ References: Section 6.01 (b)
○ From : Postponement Company Shareholder Vote - Fulsome - 6.4

+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. shall jointly prepare and file the Proxy
Statement/Prospectus and Form S-4 as promptly as reasonably practicable and no later than 30

Business Days after the Agreement date; Penumbra, Inc. shall call the Company Stockholders'
Meeting as promptly as reasonably practicable after Form S-4 effectiveness, scheduled no later
than 35 days following effectiveness, and shall use its reasonable best efforts to solicit proxies in
favor of adopting the Agreement and securing approval unless the Penumbra, Inc. Board effects
an Adverse Recommendation Change.
○ References: Section 6.01 (c)
○ From : Shareholder Vote Efforts - Fulsome - 6.5

+ Dissenting Shares receive Section 262 DGCL payment; Standard Delaware Section 262
appraisal rights apply.
○ From : Cap on Dissenting Shares - Fulsome - 6.6

Regulatory Best Efforts
+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. shall use reasonable best efforts to obtain all
required third-party consents, approvals, or waivers prior to Closing, with Boston Scientific
Corporation providing reasonable assistance, excluding obligations to compensate third parties,
litigate, or grant accommodations.
○ References: Section 6.13
○ From : Best Effort : Efforts Standard - 7.1

+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. shall cooperate to make all HSR Act filings
within 20 Business Days of the Agreement date and initiate regulatory processes within 50
calendar days, with Boston Scientific Corporation responsible for all filing fees.
○ References: Section 6.06 (a) (i)
○ From : Best Effort : HSR - 7.3

+ Boston Scientific Corporation, Penumbra, Inc., and Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc. must cooperate
to make all required foreign antitrust and foreign investment law filings and pay applicable fees.
○ References: Section 6.06 (a)
○ From : Best Effort : Foreign Filings - 7.5

+ The agreement requires Boston Scientific Corporation (“Parent”) to proffer divestitures and other
remedies to Governmental Authorities within ten months from the later of the date of Parent’s
and Penumbra, Inc.’s (“Company”) initial filings under the HSR Act, subject to Section 6.06(d) of
the Agreement and Section 6.06(d) of the Parent Disclosure Schedule. Parent and its Affiliates
may be required to hold separate, sell, license, divest or otherwise dispose of businesses or
assets, or take related actions, but only as set forth in Section 6.06(d) of the Parent Disclosure
Schedule and conditioned upon consummation of the Transactions; otherwise, Parent is not
obligated to take such actions without its prior written consent. Penumbra, Inc. may be requested
to divest, hold separate, or limit its business freedom upon Parent’s request, also conditioned on
closing. The excerpts do not specify any divestiture cap tied to revenue, assets, or sales for
Penumbra, Inc. There are carveouts limiting Parent’s obligations and these remedy actions are
not mandatory unless expressly permitted by the Parent Disclosure Schedule.
○ References: Section 6.06 (d)
○ From : Best Effort : Remedy Actions - Fulsome - 7.6

+ Boston Scientific Corporation shall have the sole right to determine, direct, and implement the
strategy for obtaining antitrust or regulatory approvals, including the unilateral decision to pull
and refile HSR filings once; any subsequent refiling requires the prior written consent of
Penumbra, Inc., with good faith consultation.
○ References: Section 6.06 (b)
○ From : Best Efforts : Right to Control Strategy - Fulsome - 7.7

+ Boston Scientific Corporation shall have the sole right to control the timing of obtaining antitrust
or regulatory approvals, including unilaterally deciding to pull and refile HSR Act filings; however,
pulling and refiling more than once requires prior written consent of Penumbra, Inc., with good
faith consultation obligations.
○ References: Section 6.06 (b)
○ From : Best Efforts : Right to Control Timing - Fulsome - 7.8
+ Boston Scientific Corporation is required to defend against any litigation.

○ References: Section 6.06 (d)
○ From : Best Efforts : Litigation Commitments - Fulsome - 7.12

+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. shall use reasonable best efforts to cooperate
on regulatory filings and approvals, avoid acquisitions or arrangements that could delay or
impede closing, and promptly address governmental objections, without altering closing
conditions.
○ References: Section 6.06 (f) (g)
○ From : Best Efforts : Acquisition Interference - 7.13
No Solicitation and Confidentiality Agreement
+ Confidentiality Agreement dated December 19, 2025.

○ References: Section 9.03
○ From : Confidentiality and Clean Room - Fulsome - 8.2

+ The Penumbra, Inc. and its subsidiaries shall not, during the Pre-Closing Period, directly or
indirectly, solicit, initiate, knowingly facilitate or encourage any Acquisition Proposal, except as
expressly permitted by Section 6.03.
○ References: Section 6.03 (b)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Go Shop Fullsome - 8.3

+ Parent is Boston Scientific Corporation, Merger Sub is Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc., and Company
is Penumbra, Inc.; Penumbra, Inc. must promptly (within one calendar day) notify Boston
Scientific Corporation in writing of Acquisition Proposals and related inquiries, disclose proposer
identities and documents, and keep Boston Scientific Corporation informed of material
developments.
○ References: Section 6.03 (c)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Notice of Competing Offer - 8.4

+ The Penumbra, Inc. shall, in person or by telephone, keep Boston Scientific Corporation
reasonably informed on a reasonably prompt basis and within one calendar day of material
developments, including changes in price, consideration, or material amendments, of any
Acquisition Proposal or related inquiries, offers, proposals, or requests; and shall promptly, within
one calendar day, notify Boston Scientific Corporation of a Superior Proposal determination by
the Penumbra, Inc. Board or its committee.
○ References: Section 6.03 (c)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Ongoing Update - 8.5

+ The board of directors of Penumbra, Inc. may effect an Adverse Recommendation Change and
terminate the Agreement prior to stockholder approval if it receives a written Superior Proposal
not resulting from a breach of Section 6.03, determines in good faith (after consultation with
outside legal counsel and financial advisors) that the Superior Proposal is superior, and that
failure to act would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties under applicable Law, following
specified notice, negotiation, and determination procedures.
○ References: Section 6.03 (e)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Adverse Change Superior Offer - 8.6

+ The Company Board of Penumbra, Inc. may effect an Adverse Recommendation Change if an
Intervening Event occurs and it determines in good faith (after consultation with outside legal
counsel) that failure to do so would likely be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties under applicable
Law, following written notice to Boston Scientific Corporation, a four-day negotiation period if
requested, and a good faith determination after considering any modifications.
○ References: Section 6.03 (e)
○ From : Non Solicitation : Adverse Change Intervening Event - 8.7

Financing
+ Committed financing was not included in the merger contract.

○ References: Section 4.11
○ From : Financing Summary : Committed Financing - Fulsome - 9.1

+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc. represent they will have "sufficient
funds" at the Effective Time to fulfill all obligations, including paying the aggregate Cash
Consideration, with no financing conditions.
○ References: Section 4.11
○ From : Financing Summary : Financing Efforts Summary - 9.2

+ Boston Scientific Corporation and Penumbra, Inc. must promptly notify each other if they receive
third-party or governmental notices, or become aware of events that could “reasonably be
expected to prevent or materially delay” the merger or cause closing-condition failure, without
imposing any obligation to seek substitute financing.
○ References: Section 6.14
○ From : Financing Summary : Substitute Financing Notice And Efforts - 9.3

Termination

+ The outside date is January 14, 2027; Boston Scientific Corporation or Penumbra, Inc. may
extend it in successive three-month increments by written notice if antitrust or Section 7.01(b) or
7.01(c) conditions remain unmet but other Article VII conditions are satisfied or waivable, with
extensions no later than January 14, 2028. Termination rights are restricted if a party’s breach
caused the delay.
○ References: Section 8.01
○ From : Outside Date - Fulsome - 10.1

+ Boston Scientific Corporation or Penumbra, Inc. may extend the Initial Outside Date in three-
month increments until January 14, 2028, and may terminate the Agreement prior to the
Effective Time except that the right to terminate shall not be available to any party whose failure
to fulfill any agreements and covenants under this Agreement has been the principal cause of, or
resulted in, the failure of the Effective Time to occur on or before such date.
○ References: Section 8.01 (b)
○ From : Extend or Terminate in Breach - Fulsome - 10.2

Termination Rights
+ The Agreement may be terminated by either Boston Scientific Corporation or Penumbra, Inc.
prior to the Effective Time: (a) by mutual written consent; (b) if the Merger has not occurred by
January 14, 2027, subject to possible three-month extensions until January 14, 2028, unless the
terminating party caused the delay; (c) if a final, nonappealable governmental order prohibits the
Merger and the terminating party has fulfilled its obligations; (d) if Penumbra, Inc. stockholder
approval is not obtained; (e) by Boston Scientific Corporation if Penumbra, Inc. effects an
adverse recommendation change; (f) by Penumbra, Inc. upon entering into a superior proposal
with payment of a termination fee; (g) by Boston Scientific Corporation for uncured material
breaches by Penumbra, Inc.; and (h) by Penumbra, Inc. for uncured material breaches by
Boston Scientific Corporation or Merger Sub, subject to specified cure periods and conditions.
○ References: Section 8.01
○ From : Termination Mutual - Fulsome - 11.1

+ The Agreement may be terminated by Boston Scientific Corporation (Parent) (i) if, prior to the
receipt of the Penumbra, Inc. (Company) Stockholder Approval, the Penumbra, Inc. Board of
Directors (Company Board) effects and does not withdraw at least five Business Days prior to
the Company Stockholder Meeting an Adverse Recommendation Change; or (ii) if Penumbra,
Inc. breaches any of its representations, warranties, covenants, or agreements under the
Agreement, such breach would cause failure of a condition in Sections 7.02(a) or 7.02(b), and
the breach is incapable of being cured prior to the Outside Date or, if curable, is not cured within
thirty calendar days after written notice from Boston Scientific Corporation or by the Outside
Date, provided Boston Scientific Corporation is not then in breach preventing satisfaction of its
conditions.
○ References: Section 8.01 (e) (g)
○ From : Termination Parent - Fulsome - 11.2

+ The Agreement may be terminated by Penumbra, Inc. (the "Company") (f) at any time before
obtaining Company Stockholder Approval if the Company Board determines to enter into an

Acquisition Agreement for a Superior Proposal per Section 6.03, provided the Company has not
breached (other than de minimis) Section 6.03 regarding the Superior Proposal and concurrently
pays Boston Scientific Corporation (the "Parent") the Company Termination Fee; or (h) if Boston
Scientific Corporation or Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc. (the "Merger Sub") breaches any
representations, warranties, or covenants that (i) cause failure of a condition in Sections 7.03(a)
or 7.03(b) and (ii) cannot be cured before the Outside Date or remain uncured thirty calendar
days after notice or by the Outside Date, provided Penumbra, Inc. is not then in breach causing
failure of conditions in Sections 7.02(a) or 7.02(b).
○ References: Section 8.01 (f)
○ From : Termination Company - Fulsome - 11.3

+ The agreement does not require The Penumbra, Inc. or Boston Scientific Corporation to
reimburse expenses specifically for termination due to failure to obtain shareholder approval.
Termination fees and reimbursements apply only under specified conditions unrelated to
shareholder vote failures. No reimbursement fees apply for failure to obtain shareholder
approval.
○ References: Section 8.03 (f)
○ From : Reimbursement Expenses Identified - Fusome - 11.5

Termination Fee Amounts
+ The Company is Penumbra, Inc., and the Parent is Boston Scientific Corporation. Company-side
termination fees are: - $525,000,000 – acquisition proposal; - $525,000,000 – recommendation
change; - $525,000,000 – vote failure.
○ References: Section 8.03 (b) (iv); Section 8.03 (b) (i)
○ From : Company Termination Fee ALL IN ONE - Concise - 12.01

Ordinary Course Covenants
+ The Penumbra, Inc. shall, and shall cause the Company Subsidiaries, to, use reasonable best
efforts to conduct the businesses of the Company Group only in the ordinary course of business
and, to the extent consistent therewith, use reasonable best efforts to preserve substantially
intact the business organization, material assets and material properties and business relations
of the Company Group, keep available the services of its executive officers and key employees
on commercially reasonable terms, maintain in effect all material licenses, permits, consents,
franchises and approvals and authorizations, and maintain satisfactory relationships of the
Company Group with any persons with which the Company Group has material business
relations and with Governmental Authorities that have jurisdiction over its business and
operations. Parent Boston Scientific Corporation is restricted from amending or otherwise
changing its Charter or By-laws or taking other specified actions during the Pre-Closing Period

except as required by applicable Law or with prior written consent of Penumbra, Inc., and must
cooperate and assist using reasonable best efforts in regulatory filings and obtaining third-party
consents.
○ References: Section 5.01 (a)
○ From : Ordinary Course Covenant - Fulsome - 13.1

MAE Definitions
+ The Material Adverse Effect carveouts in the Agreement provide that a Company Material
Adverse Effect shall not include any event, circumstance, change, condition, occurrence or effect
resulting from or relating to (i)(A) a change in general economic, political, regulatory, business,
financial, credit or capital market conditions, including interest or exchange rates or currency
fluctuations; (B) changes generally affecting the industries in which Penumbra, Inc. or its
subsidiaries operate; (C) changes in accounting requirements or GAAP or applicable Laws; (D)
disease outbreaks, epidemics, pandemics (including COVID-19), natural disasters or quarantine
restrictions; (E) changes in global or national political conditions, war, military action, sabotage,
civil unrest, terrorism; (F) announcement or pendency of the Agreement or Transactions; or (G)
compliance with the Agreement or actions requested or consented to in writing by Boston
Scientific Corporation. Further carveouts include Transaction Litigation, changes in trading price
or volume of shares, rating changes, analyst recommendations, or failure to meet projections or
forecasts, except to the extent underlying causes may be considered.
○ References: Section 9.03

○ From : MAE Standard Carveouts - Fulsome - 16.1

+ In the merger agreement among Boston Scientific Corporation, Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc., and
Penumbra, Inc., the Material Adverse Effect definition includes a carve-back whereby
disproportionate impacts on Penumbra, Inc., relative to industry peers, in exceptions (A)–(E) may
be considered only to the extent of such disproportionate impact.
○ References: Section 9.03
○ From : MAE Disproportionality - Fulsome - 16.3

+ The underlying causes of changes in trading price or volume, ratings or analyst
recommendations (clause (iii)), and failures to meet financial or operating projections or
estimates (clause (iv)) may be considered in determining whether a Company Material Adverse
Effect has occurred under the merger agreement among Boston Scientific Corporation,
Pinehurst Merger Sub, Inc., and Penumbra, Inc.
○ References: Section 9.03
